most_recent_enforcement_milestone.milestone: General-Purpose AI (GPAI) obligations and governance rules became applicable
most_recent_enforcement_milestone.notes: European Commission published final GPAI Code of Practice on 10 July 2025 to support these obligations.
sources: turn4view0 (EC FAQ on AI Act, updated 2026-03)

next_obligations_upcoming.milestone: Full application of high-risk AI obligations (Annex III) including conformity assessment, technical documentation, risk management, human oversight, post-market monitoring, CE marking for stand-alone systems and registration in EU database

key_uncertainties: Delays in harmonised standards and notified body availability (Commission FAQ notes delay; standardisation ongoing)
Many Member States had not fully designated market surveillance/supervisory authorities by late 2025, creating uneven enforcement capacity
Potential legislative changes (Digital Omnibus proposal, Nov 19, 2025) could shift application dates or adjust requirements
Definitions and scope (GPAI threshold, 'systemic risk', who counts as 'provider' and extraterritorial reach) still require Commission guidance
Enforcement prioritisation: early enforcement likely focused on most egregious violations and in Member States with operational authorities
